nativeRange.biotope: Tropical reef rubble zones, typically reported around 4–30 m depth

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped meaty foods, and high-quality marine flake or pellets. Feed several times per day given the species' active swimming. Has been documented foraging on small invertebrates including flatworms and polychaetes.

avoidWith: other male fairy wrasses in the same tank

summary: A small, brightly colored fairy wrasse from Indonesia and the Philippines, generally considered a peaceful and reef-safe addition to medium-to-large mature reef aquariums.
careNotes: Cirrhilabrus cyanogularis was formally described in 2018 and inhabits outer-reef rubble slopes from central Indonesia north into the southern Philippines, typically around 4–30 m depth. Sources report maximum size between roughly 6.2 cm SL (FishBase) and ~10 cm TL (LiveAquaria), with most retail specimens arriving at 7–9 cm. Tank-size recommendations diverge: saltwaterfish.com suggests ~190 L (50 gal) while LiveAquaria advises ~340 L (90 gal); we have leaned toward the larger end given the species' active mid-water swimming style and the value of a long footprint with open swimming lanes between rockwork. Generally peaceful and considered reef-safe; coral and ornamental-invert nipping is not commonly reported. Like most Cirrhilabrus, this species is a notable jumper — a tightly fitted lid or eurobrace is strongly recommended. Best kept as a single male, or as a male with one or more females introduced simultaneously; multiple males in the same tank tend to fight. As with other fairy wrasses, individuals are protogynous (female-first sequential hermaphrodites), and males display heightened color intensity during nuptial displays.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species page at https://www.liveaquaria.com/product/6988/ used for water parameters, tank size (90 gal), diet, temperament, and reef-safe rating.

additional.name: saltwaterfish.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (3 in / ~7.6 cm) and tank size (50 gal / ~190 L). Disagreement with LiveAquaria's 90 gal recommendation noted; averaged toward the larger value per the playbook tank-size rule.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed max length 6.2 cm SL, family Labridae, order Eupercaria, depth ~4–30 m, distribution Indonesia/Philippines/Malaysia.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Taxonomy and 2018 species description confirmed; etymology kyanos + gularis = 'blue throat'.

additional.name: Reef Builders
additional.notes: Original-description coverage; native range central-to-northern Indonesia into southern Philippines.
